His CT scans showed some changes in the sigmoid colon and vasa recta, suggesting possible inflammation or metastasis.

So, considering all this, the main issue is his persistent diarrhea.

The colon changes on CT could be due to either metastatic disease or something else, but given his history, it's more likely metastatic spread.
The fact that the pleural biopsy confirmed metastatic thymoma and that the diarrhea is unresponsive to standard treatments points towards this as the cause.
Also, the uric acid is slightly high, which can be a side effect of certain chemotherapies or due to the disease itself.
So, putting it all together, the most likely diagnosis is that the diarrhea is due to metastatic thymoma, possibly involving the colon or surrounding structures.

Putting it all together, the most likely diagnosis is that his diarrhea is a paraneoplastic manifestation of his metastatic thymoma.
Thymomas can produce a variety of substances that affect the gut, leading to diarrhea.
Alternatively, it could be related to the investigational drug, but since stopping it didn't immediately resolve the issue, the tumor itself is more likely the cause.
